meanings.definition: the choicest or most essential or most vital part of some idea or experience
examples: the gist of the prosecutor's argument
the heart and soul of the Republican Party

meanings.definition: preoccupation especially with one's attitudes and ethical or ideological values
examples: the sensitiveness of James's characters, their seeming inwardness
inwardness is what an Englishman quite simply has, painlessly, as a birthright

meanings.definition: the quality or state of being inward or internal
examples: the inwardness of the body's organs

meanings.definition: preoccupation with what concerns human inner nature (especially ethical or ideological values); - H.R.Finch
